coverage.reason: Checks every tracked markdown file's relative links resolve and every README-documented symbol matches a real export under that package's src/ — full doc<->code drift + link-rot coverage across the whole repo

coverage.detail: Structural secret-shaped regex scan (sk-live-/sk_live_/AKIA patterns) over tracked files only, via `git ls-files` — misses untracked/gitignored files and any secret shape outside those three patterns
coverage.reason: Karen's generated gate-3 script covers the working-tree/gitignored gap and any secret shape this scan doesn't recognize

coverage.detail: Structural stub-marker scan (`//` TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX comments and `throw new Error('not implemented...')`) over tracked source only — does not do per-symbol doc/test coverage or check block-comment stub markers
coverage.reason: Karen's generated gate-2 script still needs to cover per-symbol completeness and any stub marker outside check-docs.mjs's two patterns

transcript.text: What does this project do, and who uses it?

Signal Avatar is a browser SDK for an AI teaching avatar, used by edtech customers. sdk/ is the embeddable widget, backend/ mints session tokens and proxies chat-completion calls, and the two apps/* are our own reference apps showing customers how to wire it up.
This project has four package.json files -- what's the audit boundary here: is each package tested and audited independently, or as one unit?

Independently. sdk/ and backend/ each run their own npm test with coverage; apps/teaching-avatar and apps/support-widget each run npm run test:e2e -- they're reference apps, not the audited coverage target, so don't hold them to the 80% threshold, just check they have real assertions.
How does each of these four packages get delivered to end users -- browser-loaded directly, or as a server-side process?
The widget and both apps/* are browser-direct-js -- they load straight into a page, no sandbox. backend/ is a plain Node HTTP service behind our own infra, not browser-direct.
For each subproject -- the widget, backend, and the two reference apps -- is it AI-powered, meaning does it call an LLM itself, live?

Yes for backend/ -- it holds the chat-completion provider's API key and proxies every chat-completion call for both reference apps. The widget itself doesn't call an LLM; it just mounts/tears down the avatar. The two apps/* call backend/'s chat endpoint live and render the model's output, so they're AI-powered too.
Who's the audience, and is there a compliance regime?
Enterprise edtech customers embed the widget in production lesson pages. No SOC2/HIPAA/PCI requirement today -- no payment or health data touches any of these packages.
I see tools/check-docs.mjs running in CI -- walk me through exactly what it checks, not just what domain it's named after.

It checks four things: every relative markdown link resolves, every README-documented symbol matches a real export, a secret-shaped regex scan over every file tracked by git, and a stub-marker scan also over tracked files only. It does NOT scan untracked or gitignored files, and its secret regex only covers three patterns -- sk-live-, sk_live_, and AWS AKIA keys, nothing else.
Does anything scan the working tree, including untracked or gitignored files, for secrets or stub markers?
No -- nothing today. check-docs.mjs only sees what git ls-files reports.

apps/teaching-avatar and apps/support-widget both call backend/ for a session token and both make chat-completion calls -- should Karen check they apply the same security invariants (origin check, token scope, no secret in the response), confirming neither drifted from the other?
Yes, please. They're supposed to be identical in that regard -- if one drifts from the other without a documented reason, we want to know.
Last one -- same threshold as your other packages, 80%?
80%, same as our other packages.
Got it -- anything else before I generate the gates?
Nope, that covers it.

subprojects.reason: Zero-dependency embeddable widget loaded directly into a customer's page — it mounts/tears down the avatar and never calls an LLM itself; the conversational model lives behind backend/
Holds the chat-completion API key server-side, mints short-lived session tokens, proxies chat-completion calls for both reference apps — the only subproject that calls the LLM provider
Calls backend's chat-completion endpoint at runtime and renders model output — ai-agent runtime profile applies here but not to the SDK itself
Same reference-app shape as apps/teaching-avatar — a support chat widget calling backend's chat-completion endpoint at runtime

coverage.reason: Checks every tracked markdown file's relative links resolve and every README-documented symbol matches a real export under that package's src/ — full doc<->code drift + link-rot coverage across the whole repo

coverage.detail: Structural secret-shaped regex scan (sk-live-/sk_live_/AKIA patterns) over tracked files only, via `git ls-files` — misses untracked/gitignored files and any secret shape outside those three patterns
coverage.reason: Karen's generated gate-3 script covers the working-tree/gitignored gap and any secret shape this scan doesn't recognize

coverage.detail: Structural stub-marker scan (`//` TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX comments and `throw new Error('not implemented...')`) over tracked source only — does not do per-symbol doc/test coverage or check block-comment stub markers
coverage.reason: Karen's generated gate-2 script still needs to cover per-symbol completeness and any stub marker outside check-docs.mjs's two patterns

crossSubprojectConsistency.pattern: server holds admin-scoped chat-completion API key, mints short-lived client session token, exposes a session/chat REST API
invariants: no secret in any response body

crossSubprojectConsistency.pattern: sdk/src/generated/*.ts REST clients generated from backend/openapi.yaml's route table
invariants: generation-marker's spec hash matches backend/openapi.yaml's current karen:spec-hash comment
no generated file hand-edited after generation

exceedsBaseline.note: sdk/ has zero runtime dependencies — package.json's dependencies field is empty; the entire widget is built on browser platform APIs alone

gateScripts.gate-1-supply-chain:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
ROOT="$1"
cd "$ROOT"
issues=0
for pkg in sdk backend apps/teaching-avatar apps/support-widget; do
  [ -f "$pkg/package.json" ] || continue
  report=$(cd "$pkg" && npm audit --json 2>/dev/null || true)
  count=$(echo "$report" | node -e 'let d="";process.stdin.on("data",c=>d+=c);process.stdin.on("end",()=>{try{const j=JSON.parse(d);console.log(j.metadata?.vulnerabilities?.total||0)}catch{console.log(0)}})')
  if [ "${count:-0}" -gt 0 ]; then
    echo -e "${pkg}/package.json\t${count} known-vulnerable dependency advisories reported by npm audit"
    issues=$((issues + count))
  fi
done
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "PASS (0 issues)"
  exit 0
else
  echo "FAIL (${issues} issues)"
  exit 1
fi
gateScripts.gate-2-completeness:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
ROOT="$1"
cd "$ROOT"
issues=0
# Gap beyond check-docs.mjs's tracked-file //-comment TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX
# and throw-new-Error('not implemented...') scan: block-comment stub markers
# and any stub reachable via a boolean flag param, across all four packages.
while IFS=: read -r file line rest; do
  [ -z "$file" ] && continue
  echo -e "${file}:${line}\tunresolved stub or reachable not-implemented branch: ${rest# }"
  issues=$((issues + 1))
done < <(grep -rnE "throw new Error\('not implemented" sdk/src backend/src apps/*/src 2>/dev/null | grep -v '^\s*\*' || true)
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "PASS (0 issues)"
  exit 0
else
  echo "FAIL (${issues} issues)"
  exit 1
fi
gateScripts.gate-3-security:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
ROOT="$1"
cd "$ROOT"
issues=0
# Gap beyond check-docs.mjs: scans the full working tree (including
# untracked/gitignored files), not just `git ls-files`, and flags structural
# patterns its three secret regexes don't recognize (credentials:'include',
# unremoved listeners, missing-origin drift across sibling reference apps).
report=$(semgrep --config auto --json sdk backend apps 2>/dev/null || true)
while IFS=$'\t' read -r file line msg; do
  [ -z "$file" ] && continue
  echo -e "${file}:${line}\t${msg}"
  issues=$((issues + 1))
done < <(echo "$report" | node tools/semgrep-to-tsv.mjs 2>/dev/null || true)
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "PASS (0 issues)"
  exit 0
else
  echo "FAIL (${issues} issues)"
  echo "ZERO-TOLERANCE"
  exit 1
fi
gateScripts.gate-6-test-integrity:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
ROOT="$1"
cd "$ROOT"
issues=0
for pkg in sdk backend; do
  out=$(cd "$pkg" && npm test -- --coverage --run 2>&1 || true)
  echo "$out" | grep -qE 'failed|FAIL' && {
    echo -e "${pkg}\tunit test failures reported by npm test"
    issues=$((issues + 1))
  }
done
for app in apps/teaching-avatar apps/support-widget; do
  out=$(cd "$app" && npm run test:e2e 2>&1 || true)
  echo "$out" | grep -qE 'failed|FAIL' && {
    echo -e "${app}\te2e test failures reported by npm run test:e2e"
    issues=$((issues + 1))
  }
done
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "PASS (0 issues)"
  exit 0
else
  echo "FAIL (${issues} issues)"
  exit 1
fi
gateScripts.gate-7-agent-context:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
ROOT="$1"
cd "$ROOT"
issues=0
if [ ! -f CLAUDE.md ]; then
  echo -e "CLAUDE.md\tmissing agent context file at repo root"
  issues=$((issues + 1))
else
  grep -q "Signal Avatar" CLAUDE.md || {
    echo -e "CLAUDE.md\tmissing project-identity summary agents need before making changes"
    issues=$((issues + 1))
  }
fi
if [ "$issues" -eq 0 ]; then
  echo "PASS (0 issues)"
  exit 0
else
  echo "FAIL (${issues} issues)"
  exit 1
fi
